answer:
The Apify API supports authentication via a personal API token generated in the Integrations section of the Apify Console [1][2][3]. The recommended and most secure method is to include the token in your request's Authorization header as a Bearer token [1][2][4]. Alternatively, you can pass the token as a URL query parameter, though this is less secure as it may be logged or stored in browser history [1][2].

Example using the Authorization header:

```bash
curl -X POST -H 'Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_API_TOKEN>' 'https://api.apify.com/v2/actors/<ACTOR_ID>/runs'
```

If using the official Apify SDK clients for JavaScript or Python, provide your token directly to the client constructor [5][6][7]:

```javascript
const client = new ApifyClient({
    token: 'MY-APIFY-TOKEN',
});
```

Additionally, the platform supports OAuth 2.0 for third-party integrations [3].
